data.title: TOKYOPOP titles now on MangaPlaza with up to 100% point reward promo on all titles.
data.description: The manga publisher TOKYOPOP has announced that its titles are now accessible on MangaPlaza, an online platform based in Marina del Rey, Calif., and Osaka, Japan. Customers can benefit from a promotional offer of receiving up to 100% points reward on all TOKYOPOP titles purchased through the platform.

data.title: ATOM price could hit $3.64 in short term as Cosmos looks to recover from oversold levels; long-term forecast at $12.78.
data.description: Forecasts indicate that the price of ATOM could see a rebound to $3.64 in the upcoming weeks due to oversold conditions based on technical indicators. The medium-term prediction for Cosmos suggests a target range of $12.78.

data.title: Tether to nominate executives to Juventus board, 8 months post investment, aiming for stronger presence in the club.
data.description: The company behind the stablecoin has officially announced its decision to appoint individuals to the football club’s board of directors, approximately eight months following its initial financial involvement.

data.title: Norsk Hydro to release Q3 2025 results at 07:00 CEST on October 24, 2025 via hydro.com.
data.description: The financial results for Hydro's third quarter of 2025 are set to be disclosed at 07:00 CEST (01:00 EDT, 06:00 BST, 05:00 UTC/GMT) on October 24, 2025. Both the quarterly report and presentation will be accessible on the official website, hydro.com, concurrently with the announcement.

data.title: Bitcoin Cash expected to reach $575-$620 in 30 days despite being oversold with RSI at 36.17 near $477 support.
data.description: Experts are anticipating a rebound in the price of Bitcoin Cash (BCH) to reach a range between $575 and $620 in the next 30 days. The current market conditions show that BCH is nearing oversold territory, with a crucial support level at $477. The Relative Strength Index (RSI) stands at 36.17, indicating the potential for a price increase in the near future.

data.title: Over 5 new crypto ETFs filed this week, including VanEck's Lido staked Ethereum ETF and 21Shares’ leveraged Hyperliquid fund. SEC reviewing amid a government shutdown.
data.description: This week, the SEC received proposals for new ETFs, including VanEck's Lido staked Ethereum ETF and 21Shares’ leveraged Hyperliquid fund. These submissions occurred during a government shutdown period.

data.title: Amaroq Minerals unveils sales of Single Mine Origin Gold from Nalunaq mine in Greenland. #newspiece #mining
data.description: In the latest update, it has been reported that there has been a significant increase in cases of COVID-19 in the region. Over the past week, there have been 500 new cases, bringing the total number of confirmed cases to 2,000. This spike in cases has put a strain on the local healthcare system, with hospitals now operating at 90% capacity. Officials are urging the public to follow safety guidelines and get vaccinated to help curb the spread of the virus. Additionally, there have been 10 new reported deaths due to the virus, raising the total death toll to 50. The situation is being closely monitored, and further measures may be implemented to control the spread of the virus.

data.title: LGC Group invests £20 million in new National Laboratories in Guildford, enhancing UK's scientific capabilities.
data.description: LGC Group, a prominent company in life sciences, diagnostics, and analytical solutions, has launched its advanced National Laboratories facility in Guildford. Representing a significant £20 million investment, this move bolsters the UK’s scientific foundation and showcases a lasting dedication from the private sector towards the country’s research and innovation potential. This cutting-edge site consolidates three key national scientific functions within a specially structured facility.

data.title: Volvo Group Q3 2025: Net sales of SEK 110.7 billion, 1% increase adjusted for currency; Operating income SEK 11.7 billion.
data.description: In Q3 2025, the Volvo Group saw a 1% increase in net sales in Europe, but faced challenges in North and South America. The Group's total net sales were SEK 110.7 billion, down 1% from the previous year when adjusted for currency. Vehicle sales were also down 1% compared to Q3 2014. However, service sales grew by 4% when adjusted for currency, reaching SEK 126 billion over the last 12 months. Despite lower vehicle volumes, the Group maintained an adjusted operating income of SEK 11.7 billion with an operating margin of 10.6%. The net sales decreased by 5% when not adjusted for currency, but adjusted operating income stood at SEK 11,707 million, with a margin of 10.6%. A positive effect of SEK 811 million was excluded from the adjusted operating income.

data.title: Chainlink aims for $25.41 rebound from $17.31 amid bearish momentum. Forecast shows mixed signals, $16.35 crucial support for bulls.
data.description: Predictions for the LINK price point towards a potential surge to $25.41 despite its current trading value of $17.31. The Chainlink forecast shows conflicting signals, with a crucial support level at $16.35 that is essential for bullish momentum.
